avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The snowpack will remain well bonded firm and stable. Good cover will remain on most aspects above 700 metres with patches remaining at lower elevations. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: The continuing Easterly airstream will bring dry, cloudy conditions. The freezing level will be around 500 metres,  with storm force Easterly winds overnight dropping slightly during the day.
Observed Weather Influences: A dry day with gale force Easterly winds. The freezing level fell to around 700 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is well bonded firm and stable. Reasonable cover of firm snow on most aspects above 700 metres with a few patches at lower elevations. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Little change is expected with the snowpack, generally firm and stable. Isolated and shallow areas of unstable windslab will be present in very sheltered locations mainly on South-West through West to North aspects above 800 metres. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Gale force Easterly winds overnight, easing slightly during the day. It will be mainly dry with a freezing level around 500 metres.
Observed Weather Influences: Gale force Easterly winds and some very light precipitation falling as snow above 650 metres. The freezing level was around 750 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is generally firm and stable. Isolated and shallow areas of poorly bonded windslab are present in very sheltered areas mainly on South-West through West to North aspects above 800 metres. Debris from  a small slab avalanche observed on a North aspect at 1000 metres. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The existing snowpack will continue to consolidate, becoming refrozen and firm at all elevations with good stability. At higher elevations, any pockets of new windblown snow will be shallow and are not considered to be significant. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: It will be dry throughout the period with the freezing level about 500 metres. Easterly winds will remain strong to gale force, easing slightly through the day.
Observed Weather Influences: Damp and cloudy with showers falling as snow above 700 metres, some dryer and brighter spells. Very windy at higher elevations, strong to gale force from the East.
ObservedSnowStability: Following a period of consolidation overnight the snowpack was already refrozen and firm above 750 metres with good stability. Very small accumulations of fresh and redistributed snow are beginning to reform in wind sheltered locations on mainly Westerly aspects though amounts are not significant. Below 750 metres the snowpack remains softer and moist. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Little change is expected in the snowpack. Areas of well bonded windslab will remain on mainly wind sheltered West to North aspects above 900 metres. On other aspects, and below this altitude the snowpack will be shallow and firm. Stability will be good throughout.  The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: A dry but cloudy day with a freezing level of around 500 metres. Winds from the East.
Observed Weather Influences: It was a cloudy and murky but generally dry day. The freezing level rose to around 1000 metres overnight before dropping slowly to around 700 metres during the day. Winds were from the South-East.
ObservedSnowStability: The overnight temperature rise has helped to stabilise the shallow but unstable windslab deposits which were observed yesterday. Areas of windslab still remain on mainly sheltered North to East aspects above 900 metres. However, these now tend to be well consolidated and generally stable. On other aspects the snowpack tends to be firm and is stable.  The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Windslab will continue to develop with avalanches likely on West to North aspects above 800 metres. The greatest instability will be in steep locations such as gullies, corrie head walls and convexities. Cross loading will affect South-West and North-East aspects. Fragile cornices will continue to develop on the same aspects. Between 500 and 800 meters, moderately bonded windslab will develop on steep banks, convexities and burn lines. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Snowfall above 300 to 400 metres on Gale force East-South-Easterly winds.  The freezing level will be around 500 to 600 metres.
Observed Weather Influences: Strong South-South-Easterly winds with precipitation falling as snow above 600 metres. After a rise in temperature overnight the freezing level fell from 1000 to 700 metres by late morning.
ObservedSnowStability: New windslab has developed above 700 metres on Westerly to Northerly aspects, with cross loading affecting South-West and North-East aspects.  The greatest instability is above 950 metres in steep locations such as coire head walls, gullies and convexities.  Better stability between 800 and 950 metres where new accumulations are moderately well bonded. Below 800 metres the snowpack has been subject to thaw conditions; here stability is generally good.  The avalanche hazard is Considerable.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Snowfall and drifting will continue the development of moderately to weakly bonded windslab. Avalanches are likely in wind sheltered locations on West to North aspects above 900 metres. Cross-loaded deposits will also develop, with minor amounts on South but mostly on South-West and North-East aspects. Steep wind sheltered terrain below 900 metres will also be affected. Fragile cornices will continue to develop. Wind exposed slopes will be firm and icy. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: New snowfall to all levels, heaviest overnight. Strong winds throughout, predominately Easterly.
Observed Weather Influences: Snow showers throughout the period. Overnight, the freezing level rose to around the summits before lowering to 600 metres by early afternoon. Strong gale force winds from the East-South-East.
ObservedSnowStability: There has been some consolidation of recent deposits but unstable windslab is rapidly developing in wind sheltered locations on some South-West but mostly West to North aspects above 900 metres. Moderately bonded snow is also present in steep sheltered locations above 600 metres. Fragile cornices have formed above these aspects. Slopes exposed to the winds are scoured and icy.
 The avalanche hazard is Considerable.
